content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 21, 1989


Steven Bruckman
Deputy City Attorney
311 Vernon Street
Roseville, CA 95678

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-518

Dear Mr. Bruckman:

We have received your letter dated August 30, 1989 (copy
enclosed) seeking written confirmation of the telephone advice
provided to you on August 28, 1989.

This is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects cur-
rent law and the advice provided to you. Pursuant to Regulation
18530¹ (copy enclosed) adopted by the Commission on March 7, 1989,
public funds can be spent for the production and dissemination of
candidate statements in the ballot pamphlet.

Should you have any further questions regarding this matter,
do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel


By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:BMB:plh

Enclosure


¹ All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the
California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

FPPC
SEP 1 8 42 AM '89

Phone 781-0325
311 Vernon Street
Roseville, California
95678

City of Roseville
Office of the City Attorney

August 30, 1989

Blanca Breeze
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms Breeze:

On August 28, 1989, we discussed the status of California
Code of Regulations section 18530 relating to candidate
qualification statements. You explained that although the
regulation was rejected by the Office of Administrative Law on
technical grounds, it is in effect as to substance. Thus, the
city of Roseville may pay the cost of preparing and distributing
statements of candidate qualifications.

You suggested that I request a written confirmation of our
telephone conversation.

Your cooperation is appreciated.

Sincerely,

STEVEN BRUCKMAN
Deputy City Attorney

SB:pd

cc: City Clerk

September 7, 1989


Steven Bruckman
City Attorney
311 Vernon Street
Roseville, CA 95678

Re: Letter No. 89-518

Dear Mr. Bruckman:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on September 1, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Blanca Breeze an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh




428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: Pursuant to Regulation 18530 (copy enclosed) adopted by the Commission on March 7, 1989, public funds can be spent for the production and dissemination of candidate statements in the ballot pamphlet.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether public funds can be spent for the production and dissemination of candidate qualification statements in the ballot pamphlet under California Regulation 18530.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that public funds may be used to pay for the cost of preparing and distributing candidate qualification statements, as Regulation 18530 is in effect as to substance despite being rejected on technical grounds.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple copies of the incoming request letter and two FPPC responses (acknowledgment dated September 7, 1989 and substantive advice dated September 21, 1989). The substantive response letter by Kathryn E. Donovan is the primary advice letter. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers present; content extracted from letter body.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether public funds can be spent for the production and dissemination of candidate qualification statements in the ballot pamphlet under California Regulation 18530.

CONCLUSION: Pursuant to Regulation 18530 (copy enclosed) adopted by the Commission on March 7, 1989, public funds can be spent for the production and dissemination of candidate statements in the ballot pamphlet.

embedding.summary: The FPPC's General Counsel confirms written advice previously provided by telephone regarding the legality of the City of Roseville using public funds to produce and distribute candidate qualification statements in the ballot pamphlet under Regulation 18530.
